The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} are liable to submit to the fearsome reputation of the Inquisition and may be willing to answer the characters’ questions, but this won’t prevent the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} from throwing in the grenade as detailed in @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#ending-the-fight]{Ending the Fight}.

If any NPCs accompany the characters, they encourage creating a strategy before attempting to face down the gangers. Violence is the gangers’ first response. If the characters hope to win a fight against superior odds, they must be well prepared. The first step is to scout out the situation before rushing into combat. Characters can earn up to +3 Superiority, with one point each for identifying foes, examining the battlefield, and creating a good plan.

The Claymore is a rectangular structure, with only a single entrance, near what was once the cockpit. Its Zones are set up as follows:

  • The entrance area, with a few high-top tables.

  • The bar runs the length of the club, with stools on the patron side and a back area.

  • The centre of the club, where a large fireplace dominates, with a huge vent that extends through into the roof of the hive level.

  • An area of high tables and bar stools beyond the fireplace, where the scribe crouches in a back corner with three gangers standing guard.

Ending the Fight

Whenever the fight reaches a dramatic climax — such as a character suffering a @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.XTQ63WzsgBa8bGLh]{Critical Wound}, more than half of the gangers dying, or simply at the end of four Rounds — read aloud or paraphrase the following:

A small cylinder sails through one of the Claymore’s broken porthole windows. When it reaches the centre of the room, it expands into a massive iridescent sphere and then implodes to a pinpoint, drawing the air and much of the detritus in the bar into it. You feel the air sucked out of your lungs as the sphere vanishes. The gangers and Scribus Jorrgensen raise their hands in surrender as they crawl from the wreckage. The fight has gone out of them.

The characters escape injury from the implosion, but the bar is badly damaged. The gangers surrender and readily agree to answer any questions the characters may have. After about ten minutes, a squad of @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Vigilites} arrives to secure the ruined bar and capture any suspected gangers. Until then, the characters can speak with the suddenly cooperative gangers and the scribe.

Obtaining information from the survivors is an Easy (+40) Opposed Presence (Interrogation) vs Discipline (Composure) Test. The characters may attempt this test multiple times to achieve further SL. Each SL reveals two of the following through shaky teeth and dazed expressions:

  • The Scarred Hands controlled the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery} and used it as their headquarters.

  • Vigilites pressed several gangs into service at the refinery hundreds of years ago. Those gangers united to form the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands}.

  • The new ‘workers’ suffered routine chemical burns from poor working and safety conditions, which lead to their naming.

  • Knowing that an open revolt would lead to a massacre, the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} worked in the shadows, blackmailing and ‘removing’ management until the gang gained control.

  • Local @UUID[JournalEntry.WE1MLa1KVRHMD8wJ.JournalEntryPage.t5pIASspATqYLZmG]{Administratum} authorities considered this a more acceptable risk than shutting down the refinery to lose production.

  • Some of the workers too injured or infirm to continue working at the refinery took positions amongst the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph}, but were never heard from again.

  • None of the gang members nor @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.6t6rTNGmLx7VQSPn]{Jorrgensen} know what happened at the facility. All of the gangers at the site died in the blast.

  • @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Arch-Deacon Artosy} submitted requests to investigate the refinery. He also sought a security team to protect him while he offered a ‘spiritually cleansing sermon’ at the refinery.

  • The sermon took place on the day of the explosion.

After obtaining this information, encourage the characters to follow up on any other leads or rest and recuperate before they meet with the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor} in @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution}).

The Grenade

If any of the PCs are outside to see it — or quickly run outside — they see a ganger in familiar colours racing away from the site on a dirtcycle. With a Routine (+20) Lore (Voll) Test, a character recognises the colours as belonging to the Cutters gang. If present, Leo identifies the iconography without a Test.

If the characters manage to capture and question the ganger, he identifies himself as Spanner and admits he is a member of the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters}. He was told to take out @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.6t6rTNGmLx7VQSPn]{Jorrgensen}. @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.xdnPxgvr63Kg9SVL]{Blister}, a gang lieutenant, told him to do so. He assumes this is another action in their ongoing turf war against the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands}.

The Hooded Figure

The recording of the explosion retrieved from @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#augmetics-manufactorum-phi-99]{Augmetics Manufactorum Phi-99} revealed a hooded figure fleeing from the ruin of the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery} towards the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Maglev Nexus}. Based on the pict-recording and witness accounts, this person could be the purported ‘saint’, the survivor who instigated the investigation. The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Maglev Nexus} is near the site of explosion, and the characters may have already passed through it during their travels about @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Rokarth}.

The Maglev Nexus

These ancient, crumbling conveyance centres are the rotting veins of @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Rokarth}, clinging to the central Generatorium and sprawling out in all directions to link the monstrous bulk of the hive. It is a perpetual tempest of transit, with all manner of goods and workers being transported through it at all hours.

Most citizens are unaware of the loss of a single refinery — it is one of the hundreds on this level of @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Hive Rokarth} alone. When the characters begin investigating at the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Maglev Nexus}, read aloud or paraphrase the following:

The Maglev Nexus is an expansive tangle of platforms and transit lines accommodating town-sized tonnages of passengers and cargo. Each arrival and departure causes a billowing influx of biting wind as the enormous vehicles rush in and out. The smells of ozone, promethium, and unwashed Humanity are nearly overwhelming.

Crowds press together as each maglev train arrives or departs, feeding the constant flow of swarming bodies. Haulers deliver and retrieve containers of maddening size, most bearing esoteric warning symbols.

Investigating isn’t easy here — even trying to find someone to talk to in the bustling crowds represents a significant challenge. Thousands of people hurriedly pass through the station every hour, disembarking and re-embarking, never standing still. Unattended servitors keep the station running with barely tolerable inefficiency, and the characters have no means to contact those who occasionally maintain them.

There are a few islands of divergent stillness: hawkers, beggars, hustlers, and the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Vigilites} who secure the station. However, there is no guarantee that these individuals know anything about the case.

The characters may attempt as many Hard (–20) Rapport (Inquiry) Tests as they choose to — there are more than enough people to question. On failure, passers-by indicate that they do not have time to talk. On success, read or paraphrase an entry from this list:

  • (If @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.3F6ABGYbY25ViPci]{Wilkus Garn} is part of the group) ‘I’d stop asking questions if you want to keep that eye. Gangers round here will rip it right out of your skull.’

  • (If @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.foITsszSuDbwhkhE]{Casseum Calibus} is part of the group) ‘Emperor’s Eyes, keep quiet! You’re basically a walking buffet to those gangers, do you know that? They’ll chop you up for parts faster than Grox meat.’

  • (If @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.kXEct2DM0GsDCpj4]{Leo 'wink' Rantz} is with the group) ‘Looks like the gangs already got to your friend there. Always snatching people from these platforms — I’d turn to prayer. The Emperor Protects.’

  • ‘You didn’t hear it from me, but I saw them steal a Servitor just as the train doors closed. Those scum will take any opportunity.’

  • ‘I stay away from anyone with augmetics around here — the gangs snatch ‘em up off their metal feet.’

  • ‘I’d wear a hood too if any parts of my body shone in the lumen-light. Gangers are ripping people apart for a few spare solars.’

Using the Inquisitorial Seal here will attract a lot of attention, guaranteeing that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} are put on high alert and will attempt an @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#ambush!]{Ambush!}. They might even cease their kidnapping plans at the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Maglev Nexus} or try to draw the characters into a trap.

At a point you choose, the characters meet or are bumped into by a beggar named Placidia (use @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.QqMWAzDuB4RbYFVm]{Imperial Citizen}). Placidia was once a member of the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} gang and has the marks to prove it — one of his legs ends in a burnt stump, while the other is a rusty augmetic. He is sympathetic to their investigation and offers to help, with a minor catch. Every question the characters ask Placidia has a price, and he’ll shake his alms cup at the characters until he feels as though they’ve ‘donated’ enough to receive an answer (typically 10 solars each time).

Placidia will explain that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} are rarely ever at the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Nexus} and that it is de facto @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} territory. The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} are obsessed with advanced technology and regularly abduct people to steal their augmetics and sell them on underground markets. If the characters don’t suggest it, Placidia says that the best way to catch them would be to have someone stand in a conspicuous area to act as bait and draw out the kidnappers. Placidia will act as bait for 500 solars, up front, but the characters may volunteer for the dangerous task themselves or recruit someone else.

The Train Job

The @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.bB0rMr6Gez3M9bME]{Cutters Gangers} and @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.xdnPxgvr63Kg9SVL]{Blister} carefully select targets for kidnapping and know the Maglev Nexus well. They take advantage of the crowded environment and large moving trains to keep targets and witnesses oblivious to their presence.

A team of @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.bB0rMr6Gez3M9bME]{Cutters Gangers} equal to the number of characters plus @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.xdnPxgvr63Kg9SVL]{Blister} are active at the Nexus. They blend into the crowds as they scan passers-by for likely prey. Make an Opposed Routine (+20) Stealth (Hide) Test for the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters}, Opposed by the characters’ Awareness (Sight). If the characters win the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#opposed-tests]{Opposed Test}, they notice the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} surveying the station.

If the characters spot the gangers, they may immediately try to capture them for interrogation, but this won’t be easy in the crowded station. The mass of citizens will heavily restrict their movement, making every @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.kY1P9redQJjy0Oa9#zones]{Zone} Difficult Terrain which the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} will ignore, attempting to flee quickly through their territory.

If the characters have set up bait (such as Placidia), the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} will target them. If not, they target the most heavily augmented person in the station, which could include a character, at the GM’s discretion. Otherwise, use an @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.QqMWAzDuB4RbYFVm]{Imperial Citizen}. @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.xdnPxgvr63Kg9SVL]{Blister} will shoot her @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.items.Item.oRLXNHwPFJQbutAU]{Webber} at the victim, entangling them, and the other @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.bB0rMr6Gez3M9bME]{Gangers} will drag them aboard the train.

The Cutters take the bait just as a train prepares to leave the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Nexus}, starting what is effectively a combat scenario. Each train carriage is a @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.kY1P9redQJjy0Oa9#zones]{Zone}, and each train is five carriages long. The transit platform is also 5 Zones, one adjoining each carriage. The Cutters take the first Turn in Initiative order, dragging their target aboard a departing train.

As the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} strike, stress that the train has just made its last call and is about to depart the station. Every character must succeed on a Challenging (+0) Reflexes (Acrobatics) or Athletics (Running) Test on their turn to board the train on time. Characters who fail the Test must watch the train depart the station with the gangers, their victim, and any characters who passed the check aboard, or they may choose to cling to the outside of the departing train as it leaves the station, falling to the bottom of the Initiative and requiring a Hard (–20) Athletics (Climbing) to clamber aboard.

Doors connect the train’s five consecutive carriage @UUID[Compendium.impmal-core.journals.JournalEntry.hdElQAwiBr5AyoRf.JournalEntryPage.zQYkbNcsJmGintdL#zones]{Zones}, all of which sway and rock, counting as @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ACFDnnnTqHPb4nV4#difficult-terrain]{Difficult Terrain} and @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ACFDnnnTqHPb4nV4#poorly-lit]{Poorly Lit}. One ganger takes their prisoner and starts moving through the train’s Zones away from the characters. The remaining gangers stand and fight.

Ending the Fight

After 10 Rounds of combat, the maglev train arrives at its next stop. If the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} defeat the characters or hold them off, they exit the train, which may or may not end the combat. You may allow characters that missed the train to spend a point of @UUID[JournalEntry.G9oZNv929oLb66XL.JournalEntryPage.RtzvBAy6703ZWjLe#characteristics,-skills,-and-specialisations]{Fate} or 50 solars to have arrived at this maglev station before the locomotive.

In the aftermath of the fire fight, a squad of five @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Macharian Vigilite} arrive to secure the scene. If the Cutters were escaping from the characters, the Vigilites intercept and hold them. If the characters try to exert authority over the Vigilites, they must make an Opposed Presence (Leadership) Test. If the characters show their Inquisitorial Seal, the Vigilites are likely to do whatever they say.

If the characters win the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#opposed-tests]{Opposed Test}, the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Vigilites} allow them to do as they please, potentially manacling the gangers and bringing them to an interrogation room in the Precinct Fortress. If the characters lose the Opposed Test, the Vigilites simply ignore them, potentially batting them out of the way with a Shock Maul if they insist on getting in the way of ‘justice’.

Any captured Cutters will pray incessantly under their breath. A character that succeeds on a Challenging (+0) Lore (Theology) Test notices that these supplications are unusual, frequently conflating the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Blazing Seraph} with the God-Emperor in a profane way and revering the idea of change. If the characters interrogate the Cutters, they may make an Opposed Presence (Interrogate) vs Discipline (Composure) Test. The characters may make this test once for each prisoner. Reveal one of the following points for each SL:

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} abduct people to harvest their augmetics for someone called @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.7h4d6tWrLYnTC4hG]{the Armourer}.

  • @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.7h4d6tWrLYnTC4hG]{The Armourer} was the @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-hooded-figure]{hooded figure} who provided weapons for the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} in exchange for augmetics so that they could win the turf war with the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands}. The @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.7h4d6tWrLYnTC4hG]{Armourer} wasn’t abducted, merely aided in safely departing the area.

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} believe that @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.7h4d6tWrLYnTC4hG]{the Armourer} is a member of the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus}. The gangers do not know why they need to source augmetics this way.

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} often hold abductees at the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#the-chop-shop]{The Chop Shop} for the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.7h4d6tWrLYnTC4hG]{Armourer} to retrieve through a trapdoor leading to a hive maintenance tunnel system.

After obtaining this information, encourage the characters to follow up on any other leads or rest and recuperate before they meet with the Planetary Governor in @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution}. The characters might like to investigate the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#the-chop-shop]{The Chop Shop} or the @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-claymore]{The Claymore} if they haven't been there already.

Other Directions

Players often choose to take investigations in unexpected directions. When this happens, you can work with them to help craft a unique narrative you’re all interested in telling. This might mean returning to places they’ve investigated before or visiting locations not covered in this adventure. @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf]{Rokarth: A Guide to the Hive} provides a wealth of information on the sprawling vastness of @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Hive Rokarth} to aid you in this eventuality and to guide your group’s subsequent adventures.

An important note for unpredictable investigation is the conclave with the Planetary Governor in @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution}. The characters’ Patron has demanded their attendance, giving them a hard time constraint. They can’t conduct stakeouts for days on end, and visiting every part of the mind-numbingly colossal @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Hive Rokarth} that might hold a clue could take several lifetimes.

You might need to improvise with the locations and clues already detailed in this adventure. For example, the characters might use @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.kXEct2DM0GsDCpj4]{Leo's} connections to discover that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} were running @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery Delta-64} instead of visiting the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.i7jK88CnFBdOtyRd#datamill-district]{Datamill District}. However, in some cases, there might be pertinent clues the characters have missed in a particular encounter, or new evidence might prompt a new avenue of inquiry, such as the following points.

The Cathedrum Of Rationed Beneficence

Though this cathedrum enjoys a revered reputation amongst the local populace, several strange pieces of information point towards something untoward happening here, such as @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} gangers attending regular services and the spread of the peculiar beliefs of the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph}. Additionally, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Z84MuGUnnGaWzxD0]{Arch-deacon Artosy} name appears in Zhao Novem’s notes and may be mentioned when interrogating the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} gangers, potentially prompting the characters to find and question him.

@UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Part 4: Carnage At The Cathedrum} contains details about the location applicable at any time the characters might choose to investigate it. Though it is the intended site of the climax of this adventure, you shouldn’t be afraid of the characters finding out too much. If the players are getting curious and thinking for themselves, that means they’re immersed in your game and having a great time! Plus, there’s still plenty to investigate even if they infiltrate the cathedrum's sub-basement early.

The Chop Shop

The characters may have already been to the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#the-chop-shop]{The Chop Shop} to rescue @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.kXEct2DM0GsDCpj4]{Leo ‘Wink’ Rantz} (see @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#criminal-connections]{Criminal Connections}) but won’t be aware of the trapdoor connecting to the maintenance ducts beneath the building until after the battle in @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-claymore]{The Claymore}.

However, the characters’ actions may have drawn the ire of the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} — if they’ve been in more than one combat with the gang, members will be instructed to slay them on sight. The Chop Shop is their turf, and @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.xdnPxgvr63Kg9SVL]{Blister} will return to defend it with a minimum of 20 @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.bB0rMr6Gez3M9bME]{Gangers}. Characters that manage to get into the trapdoor through stealth or violence may find their way through the maintenance tunnels, leading directly to @UUID[JournalEntry.arjSGA9gcUykxq0z.JournalEntryPage.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#b.-infractionist-warrenways]{B. Infractionist Warrenways}.

Ambush!

A minor misstep is all it takes for the hunter to become the hunted in the grim darkness of the 41st Millennium. If the characters attract too much attention to themselves during their investigation (for example, by misusing the authority of their Inquisitorial Seal), their enemies amongst the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} and the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph} will attempt to slaughter them.

This ambush is intended to provide consequences for the player’s choices, but it can also serve to push the narrative forward if you feel things are moving too slowly, the players aren’t sure what to do next, or you simply want to raise the stakes.

The ambush consists of a number of @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.bB0rMr6Gez3M9bME]{Gangers}) equal to the number of characters, plus @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.xdnPxgvr63Kg9SVL]{Blister}. This ambush group will tail the characters, making a Stealth Test Opposed by the characters’ Intuition (Surroundings) or Awareness (Sight). If the characters win the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#opposed-tests]{Opposed Test}, they notice they are being followed by some rough-looking thugs wearing the gang colours of the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters}. Otherwise, the ambush group attacks, able to take a full Round of combat before the characters can retaliate.

The ambush group are zealous members of the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph}, screaming castigations such as ‘The Blazing Seraph will come! You shall not pervert the Emperor’s will!’ and will fight to the death. If inspected, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.xdnPxgvr63Kg9SVL]{Blister} (or her replacement) shows signs of corruption, the flesh near their augmetics sprouting eyes that weep blood. If any of these gangers are captured, they have the same information as the gangers encountered in @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-train-job]{The Train Job}. Their utter faith in the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Blazing Seraph} fills them with vitriol towards the characters, who they view as heretic blasphemers perverting the Emperor’s true will.

Part 3: Pious Persecution.name: Part 3: Pious Persecution
Part 3: Pious Persecution.text:
Though elevated above the teeming trillions of labour-serfs and menials that make up most of the Imperial populace, the characters are still utterly beholden to their Patron, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Oh1HWiuX3Lmk0q0l]{Inquisitor Halikarn}. They have been ordered to attend a conclave with @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor Castyx} on the third day of their investigation and have no choice but to do so. This section of the adventure details the events and consequences of the conclave.

Formal Preparations

On the eve of the second day of their investigation, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Oh1HWiuX3Lmk0q0l]{Inquisitor Halikarn} contacts the characters over vox. The tone of this conversation depends on the characters’ previous actions.

If the characters have drawn a lot of attention to themselves or misused their Inquisitorial Seal, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Oh1HWiuX3Lmk0q0l]{Inquisitor Halikarn} will sternly chastise them, informing them that their pay will be docked and that there will be further, more existentially threatening punishments if they continue to displease him. Otherwise, he will give meagre praise for their subtlety, wondering aloud if they have achieved anything and reminding them he expects a full report of what they have discovered and how it serves his goals.

@UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Oh1HWiuX3Lmk0q0l]{Inquisitor Halikarn} then informs the characters that they will be attending the conclave in the guise of members of the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#mercator-lenimen]{Mercator Lenimen}, the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#mercator-lenimen]{Chem Guild}. Clothes and identification scrolls suitable for such a cover have already been delivered to the characters’ hab in @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.i7jK88CnFBdOtyRd#thaler-hostelry]{Thaler Hostelry}. They should clean themselves and rest before journeying through the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Maglev Nexus} to @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#castyx]{House Castyx} tomorrow morning. He also warns them that exposing themselves as members of the Inquisition to the assembled conclave would cause dire harm to his wider operations and could result in their immediate termination (whether @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Oh1HWiuX3Lmk0q0l]{Halikarn} means termination of employment or their lives is unclear).

When the characters decide to head to the conclave at @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#castyx]{House Castyx}, read or paraphrase the following:

You ride in a maglev carriage for what feels like hundreds of vertical miles for more than an hour, before the plasteel box judders to a halt. The thick industrial doors screech open slowly, revealing a full row of Macharian Vigilites bedecked in the additional heraldry of House Castyx, pointing Autoguns and Webbers at your group.

Seeing your clothes and ident-credential scrolls, the Vigilites sternly motion for you to follow them, surrounding you as they lead you through an open concourse surrounded by once-gilded, now heavily corroded statuary. For the first time in days, you feel sunlight and breathe unrecycled air — though both are tainted by the murky sea of smog punctuated by gothic spires.

The Vigilites escort you up winding stairways towards what initially looks like a massive wall, shrouded by the acrid fumes, but gradually reveals itself to be a colossal statue, inset with numerous arched windows. You can almost make out the entirety of a colossal shield held by the statue, bearing the unmistakable sigil of House Castyx. You’re led through a door into the multistorey-high toe of the statue, down hallways of faded grandeur, including one that appears to be a massive cannon barrel.

At this point, ask all of the characters to make a Routine (+20) Discipline (Composure) Test. Any characters that fail are @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ACFDnnnTqHPb4nV4#frightened]{Frightened} by members of @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#castyx]{House Castyx} until they successfully pass any Test. The aggressive security measures and imposing architecture are designed to keep the noble Highborn of Rokarth safe, showcase their devotion to the Emperor, and intimidate any guests to demonstrate their power. As they continue, read or paraphrase the following:

Finally, you arrive in a cavernous hall. Central is a large round table crafted of gilded ceramite, which looked like it might have once served as a command station aboard a voidship. Surrounding the table are mouldering velveteen chairs. Cherubs fly among the decaying frescoes on the distant ceiling, weaving between massive statuary as they play scratchy recordings of Ecclesiarchy hymns.

You are announced by vox-servitors with gilded augmetics, who proclaim in buzzing monotone ‘The Mercator Lenimen Delegation’. The only two people at the table look up at you: one wears an ostentatiously furred cloak embroidered with obscure litanies, pinned together with a broach bearing the crest of House Castyx. Beside the man you assume to be the Planetary Governor is a burly woman wearing customised Macharian Vigilite armour, replete with undamaged but time-worn gilding and the emphasised symbology of House Castyx.

Managing A Full Cast

Running five NPCs in one scene might sound challenging for you as the Gamemaster, but it’s easier than it sounds. By this point in the adventure, you’re already experienced enough to take on anything. But if you’re feeling intimidated by the task, move on to @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#the-prosecution]{The Prosecution}, a more structured scene where it is unlikely that anyone except for the characters and @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Arch Deacon Artosy} will talk.

If you plan to run @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#meeting-the-conclave]{Meeting The Conclave}]{Meeting the Conclave}, consider having the NPCs enter one at a time. Whenever the characters are finished talking to an NPC, or you feel the conversation drawing to a close, you can have the servitors announce the arrival of the next NPC. This way, you never have to worry about a complex five-way conversation, and the characters can learn the interests of everyone present, which might help them craft their arguments during @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#the-prosecution]{The Prosecution}.

This approach provides the perfect opportunity for the characters to meet each attendee individually to get a measure of them. Try to distinguish how you roleplay each NPC by changing your accent, style of speech, posture, and word choice, consider their dispositions and goals. Refer to the NPC notes under @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#meeting-the-conclave]{Meeting The Conclave}]{Meeting the Conclave} whenever you need to.

The characters are the first to arrive at the conclave and don’t need to make any Tests to know that they are in the presence of @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor Jaspar Castyx} and the head of the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Macharian Vigilites}.

@UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor Castyx} is almost as busy as he is powerful and wishes to handle this matter as quickly as possible — he is reviewing scrolls and dataslates on tithe inputs even as the characters arrive and isn’t particularly interested in meeting them. His bodyguard is the head of the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Macharian Vigilites} in @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Rokarth}, @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Probator-Senioris Arractan Tarsk}, who glares at the characters while appraising them as a potential threat against her charge.

Meeting the Conclave

This conclave is being held by @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor Castyx} to discern the truth behind the explosion of @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery Delta-64}, gathering important individuals with interests and responsibilities on the hive level. The following is a list of the attendees, including their disposition, agenda for the conclave, and roleplaying guidance. @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#castyx]{Castyx} and @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Tarsk} are already present, and the rest of the attendees will arrive within an hour.

Planetary Governor Jaspar Castyx

Disposition: Feigned joviality masking haughtiness, irritability, and impatience.

Agenda: To supply the tithe at any cost and deal with this minor inconvenience as quickly as possible.

Roleplaying Guidance: Governor Castyx is the ruler of @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.K9f5dlvEMBE2fYeW]{Voll} and is aware of the colossal power he wields and the responsibility he bears. His heritage is imperious rulership and the ruthless profiteering of a @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#rogue-trader-dynasties]{Rogue Trader Dynasty}. Castyx views all factions as a means to an end and every representative as replaceable.

Castyx tries to appear jovial to put people at ease but is a cunning, calculating, and serious man. He tends to slouch as though the enormity of his position is literally weighing down on him and speaks in a commanding tone through a fake smile.

Probator-Senioris Arractan Tarsk

Disposition: Suspicious and sombre. Distrustful of everyone but @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#castyx]{Castyx}, especially the characters. Agenda: To keep Governor Castyx safe, advance the status of the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Macharian Vigilites}, and reduce any advantage @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#infractionists]{Infractionists} might have in @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Rokarth}. Roleplaying Guidance: Tarsk’s first loyalty is to the Planetary Governor, and she is ever vigilant to any threat to his person. Even when she is almost certain he is safe, she will attempt to intimidate anyone near her charge. She is grimly determined and rose to her lofty position through her dedication and propensity for brutal violence. Tarsk is truly pious, believing that she owes her fortune in surviving and thriving in the dangerous @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Hive Rokarth} to the God-Emperor, and she reveres the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum}. Tarsk has a stern demeanour, seems to be permanently standing to attention, and growls any sentence that isn’t a barked order.

Mechasapient Hex-Sigma Numenon

Disposition: Endlessly curious and incredibly logical. Attempts to come across as emotionless, but spirals into zealous anger and existential fear when accused during @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#the-prosecution]{The Prosecution}.

Agenda: To refute the ‘miracle’ and find the truth behind the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery} explosion, promote the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus}, and learn more about the advanced technologies circulating in the hive.

Roleplaying Guidance: A devout member of the Machine Cult, Hex-Sigma has extensive augmetics but is more concerned with the pursuit of recovering knowledge than replacing the weakness of the flesh. She represses almost all her emotions, as is common amongst her creed, but cannot hide her passion for information. She is very interested in the rumours of strange weaponry in the hands of gangers, and is utterly oblivious to how suspicious such interests may make her. Her beliefs put her at odds with the Ministorum, and she finds the idea of a ‘miracle’ illogical.

The weight of Numenon’s augmetics makes her hunched, and she speaks in a drawling monotone unless her interest is piqued by machinery.

Administratum Overseer Jamine Lou

Disposition: Snooty, irritable, stressed, and twitchy.

Agenda: To express their dissatisfaction with the decreased productivity and the activities of the Adeptus Mechanicus and the Adeptus Ministorum.

Roleplaying Guidance: Overseer Lou is an exemplary servant of the Administratum, a fastidious record keeper and a meticulous, uncompromising manager of the millions of bureaucratic tasks taking place daily on her designated level of @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Hive Rokarth}. Though Lou is utterly devoted to the Imperium and the Emperor, she privately sees all forms of extensively organised worship — both the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} and the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} — as potentially wasted time. However, the implementation of labour-cathedrums in @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Rokarth} pleases her immensely, and she bears begrudging respect for @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Arch-Deacon Artosy}.

Lou moves almost constantly, her mind buzzing with calculations on wasted seconds. She speaks in a fast, efficient patter in a high-pitched, nasally voice, frowning at whoever she is speaking to and often cutting them off mid-sentence in pursuit of more efficient conversation.

Using the Inquisitorial Seal

Though their Patron warned them against using their Inquisitorial Seal at the conclave, the characters may reason that these are dire enough circumstances to do so regardless. In this eventuality, the conclave falls deathly silent. @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#castyx]{Castyx} speaks privately with @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Tarsk}, then allows the conclave to continue. @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} will present his case with hesitation. If he is not imprisoned by@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#the-governors-verdict]{The Governor's Verdict}, he will hasten the ritual in @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Part 4}, or may attempt to flee with the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph}, leaving the investigation at a dead end. @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Oh1HWiuX3Lmk0q0l]{Halikarn} will be furious with the characters, as detailed in @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#conclave-aftermath]{Aftermath}.

Arch Deacon Excavus Artosy

Disposition: Smugly content, talkative, and seemingly in a permanent state of pious bliss.

Agenda: To prove that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} are in league with the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} gang and are responsible for the explosion of @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery Delta-64}.

Roleplaying Guidance: Artosy is defined by the contrast between his public and private life. In public, he serves as the potentate of the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Cathedrum of Rationed Beneficence}, enjoying popularity as a deeply pious man that aids the labourers with alms and supplies the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} with abundant labour. In private, Artosy has fallen for the deceptions of the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph}; his corrupted mind is now bent to the worship and protection of a daemon. Artosy intends to hide the heretical truth behind the Coven of the Blazing Seraph as it grows in popularity and to disguise its connections to himself and the Cutters gang. To this end, he has twisted the investigation into the site of the ‘miracle’ and will use this conclave to present a case damning the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} and shifting focus away from the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters}.

Artosy often stares dazed into the middle distance as though seeing some holy vision, but is friendly, talkative, and genuinely interested in the characters. His voice is lilting, soft, and peppered with reverent references to prayers and litanies.

The Prosecution

Once all the attendees of the conclave have arrived and you feel that the characters have had enough opportunity to speak with them and understand their roles, read or paraphrase the following:

Planetary Governor Castyx clears his throat and motions for all present to array themselves for a conclave as cherubim swoop around the room blaring official dirge-hymns. All attendees move quickly to obey the governor before he nods curtly to Arch-Deacon Artosy and says, ‘It is high time this matter was dealt with, that we may all return to our duties in providing the Emperor’s Tithe. Venerable Arch Deacon Artosy, please present the findings of your investigation. Was this explosion a miracle?’.

All eyes turn to the Arch Deacon as he steps forward bearing a grave expression, speaking in a sombre tone: ‘I fear not, governor. Though it pains me no end to make such an accusation, I believe that the destruction of Acid Manufactorum Delta-64 resulted from nefarious sabotage and that one amongst us is to blame. I will present the findings of my investigation and allow this conclave to come to its conclusions. Though an open mind is like a fortress with its gate unguarded, we must be ever vigilant to enemies amongst us.’

The gathered conclave descends into overlapping shocked arguments before the planetary governor holds up a hand calling for silence and speaks commandingly: ‘We shall hear the arch deacon’s arguments. Then you may each advise me as you feel necessary before I decide what is to be done.’

Pious Persecution

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} has no physical evidence and relies solely on appeals to authority and somewhat logical arguments. If pressed or cornered by the characters’ arguments, he has little to fall back on, potentially claiming that he ‘saw the events in dream visions sent by the Emperor’, ‘found the truth through dedicated prayer’, ‘followed a divination of the Emperor’s Tarot’ or simply refuses to reveal his confidential sources.

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} will present his findings, composed of the following three major points: the Motive (Insufficient Augmetics), the Means (Dangerous Technology), and the Opportunity (Tech-Priest Involvement). After @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} has presented each point, allow the characters to interrupt with their counterpoint arguments and evidence. The other NPCs present may ask questions of the characters or interject with their points, though @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Governor Castyx} will remain silent.

The simplest way to run this encounter is to call for an @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#opposed-tests]{Opposed Test} at the end of each of the three major points, typically a characters’ Presence (Leadership) or Rapport (Charm) vs @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy’s} Charm (Deception). If the characters argue their points well, you may award them bonus SL on the Test. Keep track of how many points the characters won or lost, and after all three arguments have been resolved, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Governor Castyx} will believe whichever side won the most Tests (see @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#the-governors-verdict]{The Governor's Verdict}).

Remember that though @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Governor Castyx} will make the final decision, he can be influenced by all of the NPCs present and may decide he has heard enough discussion on a piece of evidence at any time, allowing you to control the pace of the encounter. Conversely, if your players are engaged by roleplaying a full courtroom drama, you might like to extend this scene, asking for more Tests as the characters attempt to convince all parties present or even cast aspersions on @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy}.

Motive (Insufficient Augmetics)

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} argues that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} had a motive to commit the crime with the following points:

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} are struggling to meet the demand for augmetics in @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Hive Rokarth}.

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} investigation discovered @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} gang graffiti near the Acid Refinery and the neighbouring Augmetics Manufactorum.

  • @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} believes the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} recruited the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} to abduct citizens and steal their augmetics so that the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-core.actors.Actor.rilaQaBPslIRzGco]{Tech-Priests} could claim they were meeting their quota.

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} investigation suggested that the explosion resulted from a turf war between the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} and the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters}, possibly instigated by the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus}.

Overseer Lau will support @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy’s} claim that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} are struggling to meet their augmetics quota. @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Probator-Senioris Tarsk} will support @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy’s} claim that gangers are abducting people and stealing their augmetics.

Potential Counterpoints

The characters likely have evidence that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} controlled the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery} if they investigated the @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-administratum-roster]{The Administratum Roster}, which may also inculpate @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} in the explosion. They may also know that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} had significantly more advanced equipment than the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands}. Making any of these arguments could earn the characters +1 or more SL on their @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#opposed-tests]{Opposed Test}.

Means (Dangerous Technology)

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} argues that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} had the means to commit the crime with the following points:

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} have unrivalled access to advanced technology and weaponry.

  • There are rumours throughout @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn]{Rokarth} that gangers have access to advanced technology.

  • The ruins of @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery Delta-64} are enveloped in some kind of strange glow, likely the result of advanced technology.

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} could have supplied the Scarred Hands gang with advanced technology in exchange for providing them with stolen augmetics.

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Probator-Senioris Tarsk} will support @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy’s} claim that gangers have advanced technology.

Potential Counterpoints

The characters may have evidence that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} were the ones with advanced technology, and from first-hand accounts, they may know that the wargear in question was poorly made — something the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} would never allow. They may also know that the person providing the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} with advanced technology is a heretek known as the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.7h4d6tWrLYnTC4hG]{Armourer}. The characters might also know that the explosion at the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#acid-refinery-delta-64]{Acid Refinery} was due to a disruption of the refinery’s plasma generatorium rather than any external equipment. Making any of these arguments could earn the characters +1 or more SL on their @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#opposed-tests]{Opposed Test}.

Opportunity (Tech-Priest Involvement)

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} argues that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} had plentiful opportunity to commit the crime, citing the following points:

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#adeptus-mechanicus]{Adeptus Mechanicus} are a secretive and mysterious organisation.

  • There are accounts of a @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-hooded-figure]{hooded figure} fleeing from the scene of the explosion.

  • The @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-hooded-figure]{hooded figure} could not have fled from the explosion without advanced technology, so they must have been a Tech-Priest.

  • The nearby @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Maglev Nexus} has been the site of multiple abductions of citizens with augmetics.

  • There are accounts of the @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-hooded-figure]{hooded figure} meeting with @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} gangers at the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Maglev Nexus} after the explosion.

Potential Counterpoints

The characters may know that the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} were active at the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.ciGIDGrZqWnhhZSn#maglev-nexus]{Maglev Nexus}. They may even have reports that some were seen in the company of the hooded person and that the individual in question was likely the heretek known as the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.7h4d6tWrLYnTC4hG]{Armourer}. Further, they might know that @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} gave a sermon on the day of the attack, which drew security away from the refinery. Making any of these arguments could earn the characters +1 or more SL on their @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#opposed-tests]{Opposed Test}.

The Governor's Verdict

After all arguments and Tests have been resolved, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Governor Castyx} calls for silence and then may confer with other NPCs present individually. You may allow the characters to attempt to talk to the Planetary Governor privately (though @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Probator-Senioris Tarsk} will not leave his side under any circumstances).

Count up the number of arguments the characters won and consult this table for the results:

Arguments WonResult
3@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#complete-refutation]{Complete Refutation}
2@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#strong-counterargument]{Strong Counterargument}
1@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#weak-counterpoint]{Weak Counterpoint}
0@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#pointless-interruption]{Pointless Interruption}

Pointless Interruption

@UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor Castyx} orders for @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#mechasapient-hex-sigma-numenon]{Hex-Sigma Numenon} to be detained, pending further investigation into the matters raised. @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#mechasapient-hex-sigma-numenon]{Numenon} goes quietly, muttering calculations under her breath. @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Governor Castyx} dismisses the conclave, ordering everyone to leave so that he can proceed to his next vital appointment.

Overseer Lau will spread word of the characters’ inept arguments and unconscionable time wasting, causing them to suffer −1 Influence with the Administratum.

Additionally, the characters’ strange but unfocused knowledge piques the suspicions of @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Probator-Senioris Tarsk}, who suspects they may be spies of some sort with falsified identities. She directs her faction to be wary of the characters, causing the group to suffer −1 Influence with the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Macharian Vigilites}.

Worst of all, @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} returns to the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Cathedrum} triumphant and will begin the ritual as detailed in @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Part 4: Carnage At The Cathedrum}.

Weak Counterpoint

This result is the same as for @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#pointless-interruption]{Pointless Interruption}, above, but the group do not lose any Influence.

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Probator-Senioris Tarsk} will approach the characters as they leave and take them to a private sub-chamber. She will attempt to interrogate the characters, trying to get them to reveal their true identities. Call for a Discipline (Composure) Test, Opposed by Tarsk’s Presence (Interrogation). For this encounter, Tarsk uses the statistics of a @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Macharian Vigilite}.

If the characters win the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#opposed-tests]{Opposed Test}, @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Tarsk} grunts and says that she’ll be watching them before mentioning offhand that the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor} can’t be seen investigating the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} for political reasons, even if there might be something to their weak arguments. If the characters lose the Opposed Test, her expression will drop as she realises the gravity of the situation — Inquisition involvement is a terrifying prospect, and Tarsk will simply leave, though word of this will reach @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Oh1HWiuX3Lmk0q0l]{Halikarn} with consequences.

Strong Counterargument

@UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor Castyx} orders for both @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Arch Deacon Artosy} and @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#mechasapient-hex-sigma-numenon]{Hex-Sigma Numenon} to be detained, pending further investigation into the matters raised. @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#mechasapient-hex-sigma-numenon]{Numenon} goes quietly, muttering calculations under her breath. @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} is shocked, protesting loudly and denouncing all present in the name of the Emperor and the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Blazing Seraph}. @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Governor Castyx} dismisses the conclave, ordering everyone to leave so that he can proceed to his next vital appointment.

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#probator-senioris-arractan-tarsk]{Probator-Senioris Tarsk} will approach the characters as they leave and take them to a private sub-chamber. Tarsk will make a half-hearted attempt to grill the characters to figure out how they gathered their information, before telling them she is suspicious that the characters aren’t who they say they are, but that she respects their investigative abilities. She then begrudgingly informs them that @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Planetary Governor Castyx} was somewhat impressed by their arguments at the conclave and asks that they continue to investigate on his behalf — he can’t be seen to challenge the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} directly. He would appreciate it if they could get to the bottom of this strange case. Tarsk gives the characters a vox frequency they can use in emergencies.

A @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Macharian Vigilite} is on call to aid the characters for the remainder of the adventure and may be a helpful ally in @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Part 4: Carnage At The Cathedrum}. You might like to have the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.O49jTi2TzoFQH5GT]{Macharian Vigilite} be someone the characters have met before, such as @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#squad-leader-baranoth]{Baranoth} or @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#duty-officer-vogth]{Vogth}. Additionally, the group gains +1 Influence with the Macharian Vigilites.

Complete Refutation

This result is mostly the same as @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution}, but @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.iMGPD9w3Yzerz2g6]{Governor Castyx} does not order for @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#mechasapient-hex-sigma-numenon]{Hex-Sigma Numenon} to be detained.

The @UUID[Compendium.impmal-core.actors.Actor.rilaQaBPslIRzGco]{Tech-Priest} becomes uncharacteristically emotional, profusely thanking the characters for their support, fully recognising that she would have met a much worse fate without their intervention. @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#mechasapient-hex-sigma-numenon]{Numenon} gives the characters her vox frequency and tells them that though she is indeed struggling to meet her quotas, she can lend them support if needed. @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#mechasapient-hex-sigma-numenon]{Numenon} becomes a contact for each member of the group.

This crushing victory has positive consequences for @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Part 4: Carnage at the Cathedrum}. The @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph} is unguided without @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy’s} leadership. If the characters initiate combat beneath the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Cathedrum of Rationed Beneficence}, award them +1 Superiority for demonstrating their ability to undermine the plans of one of the Coven’s major players by removing a leader from the fight.

Conclave Aftermath

After the conclave, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Oh1HWiuX3Lmk0q0l]{Halikarn} will vox the characters and demand a report. Regardless of the result, the group’s Patron will insist they continue their investigation, focusing on @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Arch-Deacon Artosy} and any clues connected to him, which should lead the characters to @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Part 4: Carnage at the Cathedrum}.

Part 4: Carnage At The Cathedrum.name: Part 4: Carnage At The Cathedrum
Part 4: Carnage At The Cathedrum.text:
This section of the adventure revolves around the characters infiltrating the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Cathedrum of Rationed Beneficence} to uncover the truths connecting the explosion at the acid refinery, the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Cult of the Blazing Seraph}, and @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Arch Deacon Artosy}. However, the characters might journey to the cathedrum before the conclave in @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution}. This section will aid you in running both situations.

Cathedrum Status

The status of the cathedrum, its attendants, and the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Cult of the Blazing Seraph} below, is dependent on what has happened so far in the adventure. Whenever the characters arrive at the cathedrum, consider the following questions:

Has the conclave in @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution} occurred?

If the answer is yes, go to the next question.

If the answer is no, @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Z84MuGUnnGaWzxD0]{Archdeacon Artosy} is likely either leading a congregation or will meet the characters as they enter the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#sanctum-majors]{Sanctum Majoris}, having heard reports of them from Galliach Krane (See @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#adeptus-ministorum-investigators]{Adeptus Ministorum Investigators}). He will behave as detailed in @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#meeting-the-conclave]{Meeting The Conclave}.

Was Artosy captured during @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution}?

If the answer is yes, the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} are in a state of slight disarray. The characters will be allowed to come and go from the cathedrum as they please, and may find it much easier to find a way into the catacombs. Additionally, the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph} are also panicking without their leader — their Resolve decreases by 1, and they attempt to hasten the ritual in the @UUID[JournalEntry.arjSGA9gcUykxq0z.JournalEntryPage.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#e.-torments-cloister]{Torment's Cloister}, sacrificing one of their number to ensure the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.526o94ox7s2Wqbwo]{Accursed Torment} is summoned, ensuring both the @UUID[JournalEntry.arjSGA9gcUykxq0z.JournalEntryPage.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#c.-forbidden-temple]{Forbidden Temple} and the @UUID[JournalEntry.arjSGA9gcUykxq0z.JournalEntryPage.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#e.-torments-cloister]{Torment's Cloister} are Warp-Touched.

If the answer is no, then @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} has instructed the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} to intercede on any attempts made by the characters to investigate the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Cathedrum}. They will attempt to prevent the characters from entering the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#cathedrum-tombs]{Cathedrum Tombs} or the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#processing-chambers]{Processing Chambers}, but will certainly falter if the Inquisitorial Seal is presented. However, these priests know little to nothing of the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph}, and are willing to sacrifice their lives to preserve the sanctity of the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#cathedrum-tombs]{Cathedrum Tombs}, interceding on any attempts to damage the hallowed halls and break into the sub-tombs.

@UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} is frantically trying to bring his plans forward in the @UUID[JournalEntry.arjSGA9gcUykxq0z.JournalEntryPage.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#e.-torments-cloister]{Torment's Cloister}, having successfully created one @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.526o94ox7s2Wqbwo]{Accursed Torment} and is deep in the process of creating another.

In either case, if the characters take more than a day to investigate the cathedrum, the ritual is completed with dire consequences. The @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.526o94ox7s2Wqbwo]{Accursed Torment} emerges through the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#cathedrum-tombs]{Cathedrum Tombs}, enters the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#sanctum-majors]{Sanctum Majoris}, and attempts to cause as much carnage as possible as the rest of the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph} — including the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.7h4d6tWrLYnTC4hG]{Armourer} — escape through @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-chop-shop]{The Chop Shop} Tunnels. They may leave many clues behind, but the group will have to hunt the fleeing heretics down into the Bowels of Rokarth to fulfil their duty.

Establishing Superiority

Depending on the clues the characters have found thus far, they may have earned @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#superiority]{Superiority} (see the @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.kY1P9redQJjy0Oa9]{Basic Rules Reference Sheet}). As Gamemaster, it’s up to you to determine how much Superiority the group has earned, but it's worth considering the following points while keeping in mind the maximum @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#superiority]{Superiority} before combat begins is 3.

Know Thy Foe

The characters have almost certainly fought against the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} before this point, and are aware of their unstable weapons and tactics, earning them +1 @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#superiority]{Superiority}. However, if the characters have not yet faced a mutated member of the @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph} (such as during an @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#ambush!]{Ambush!}) or gained the information that they are facing such a terrifying foe, they will lose 1 Superiority when they finally encounter them.

Know Thy Battlefield

If the characters infiltrate the catacombs through the @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-chop-shop]{The Chop Shop}, you might award them @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#superiority]{Superiority} as they are more familiar with the environment. Otherwise, the characters will have to come up with something clever, perhaps by acquiring the blueprints of the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Cathedrum of Rationed Beneficence} at the @UUID[.jmu38RAWes3N5Ags#the-administratum-roster]{Datamill}.

Know Thy Players

Interrogating the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} or @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Z84MuGUnnGaWzxD0]{Archdeacon Artosy} might earn the group @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.5pxNe7mQlIwonJQL#superiority]{Superiority}, if they can find information they can tactically exploit. Gaining any form of fire support from @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution} does not confer Superiority (the extra firepower is enough of a bonus!) but if Macharian Vigilites are supporting the group, they might have experience battling specific foes or could scout ahead for the characters, earning them Superiority.

The Cathedrum of Rationed Benefience

However the characters arrive at the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy]{Cathedrum}, read or paraphrase the following:

‘A clamouring crowd bustles around the base of the Cathedrum of Rationed Beneficence, filling the well-worn steps that lead to its gargantuan doors, once gilded, now streaked with stains from acid rain. The broad columns of the cathedrum are carved into figures writhing with worship, supporting one another and appearing as an idealised vertical representation of the pious mob before you. The many steeples of the cathedrum, each in a differing state of disrepair, threaten to scrape the ceiling of this level of the hive, and you cannot grasp the full bulk of the massive building. Indeed, it seems as though its foundations are actually far below this habway.

As you approach, organ driven hymnals intermingle with the clamour of the crowd, and the cloying scent of incense wafts over the tangy stench of rations being handed out by Adeptus Ministorum priests on the Steps of Beneficence.’

Steps of Beneficence

This grand staircase leads to the single entrance of the cathedrum, and is thronged at all hours by hivers requesting alms, with @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} preachers dispersed amongst them, distributing nutrient-pastes and giving half-hearted benedictions. These folk have little knowledge on anything pertaining to the investigation, but have similar attitudes to the @UUID[.XWjIkLBokiwV20Dr#injured-labourer]{Injured Labourer} at the Acid Refinery:

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#scarred-hands]{Scarred Hands} are dangerous criminals that must be eradicated.

  • The @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#cutters]{Cutters} are frightening and unpredictable, but as they are pious they may be trustworthy.

  • The @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph} are well intentioned extremists.

  • @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Z84MuGUnnGaWzxD0]{Archdeacon Artosy} is a fine leader of the most generous cathedrum.

You may allow the group to make a Hard (−20) Awareness (Sight) or Logic (Investigation) Test to notice that some of the most bedraggled beggars on the steps are furtively sneaking away from the steps and @UUID[JournalEntry.arjSGA9gcUykxq0z.JournalEntryPage.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#b.-infractionist-warrenways]{Infractionist Warrenways}.

Sanctum Majors

When the characters enter the Sanctum Majoris, read or paraphrase the following:

‘As you squeeze through the stinking crowd and push into the cathedrum proper, you are struck by the enormity of cathedrum interior. The far end of the Sanctum Majoris is misty in the distance, graced by a faded fresco of Saint Solar Macharius blocked only by air thick with incense smoke. A few small clouds of caustic rain float below the domed ceiling, and it seems as though every surface has been re-carved to represent some minor saint as part of continuous repairs.

Smaller shrines are distributed throughout this grand chamber amongst a maze of statuary depicting righteous acts, with the great and pious of the Lower Hive milling between them to meet in private prayer nooks, tombs, or attend voxamplified sermons, their zealotry overlapping in the echoey space. You see a few folk climb ladders attached to carven pillars to reach unstable choir-lofts, as others descend skull-lined staircases.

Ahead of you, a member of the Adeptus Ministorum is guiding visitors — those who seem well dressed or important are ushered through into the main body of the Sanctum Majoris, whilst ragged labourers are either directed back outside, or hurried into a side door.’

If @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Z84MuGUnnGaWzxD0]{Archdeacon Artosy} is here (@UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#cathedrum-status]{Cathedrum Status}), see @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#meeting-the-archdeacon]{Meeting The Archdeacon}. Otherwise, they are met by an @UUID[JournalEntry.pVOsgxlhuYl8D1IJ.JournalEntryPage.ZRtK93OqwboVMET3#adeptus-ministorum]{Adeptus Ministorum} preacher named Nijab, who will assume that they are here to worship at the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#processing-chambers]{Processing Chambers}, directing them to follow the other folk that seem unimportant. If @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.DMbhyUaSXwMJ44Zq]{Sister Helza Scorn} is part of the group, Nijab will instead direct them into the Sanctum Majoris proper. Though you may allow the characters to explore as they wish and improvise any encounters they may have, there are two locations you should be aware of: the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#processing-chambers]{Processing Chambers}, as previously mentioned, and the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#cathedrum-tombs]{Cathedrum Tombs}, accessible via the skull-lined staircases.

Solar Ceremonies

As Archdeacon, @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ#arch-deacon-excavus-artosy]{Artosy} may call for a Solar Ceremony, a pious observance of the great deeds of Saint Macharius, whenever he wishes. During a Solar Ceremony, the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#processing-chambers]{Processing Chambers} fall silent, as the workers are invited to pray alongside the main congregation in the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#sanctum-majors]{Sanctum Majoris}.

This is a long-winded, rambling sermon that fills the faithful with zeal and the indifferent with boredom aplenty. The players may recognise snippets from obscure histories and dry sacred texts, interspersed with the repetition singing of hymns to eternal glory of the Emperor.

To attend such a function is considered a great blessing for all. However, there are some who do not attend the Solar Ceremonies. The @UUID[.XY6whksmB72I3xNS]{Coven of the Blazing Seraph}, including @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Z84MuGUnnGaWzxD0]{Archdeacon Artosy}, use the Solar Ceremonies as an opportunity to enter the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#the-catacombs]{Catacombs} via the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#processing-chambers]{Processing Chambers}.

The Cogregation

When full, the cathedrum is a host of faithful worshippers numbering in the thousands. Though most who come to worship at the cathedrum are strongly encouraged to toil in the God-Emperor’s name in the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#processing-chambers]{Processing Chambers}, those of the highest status in the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.i7jK88CnFBdOtyRd]{Lower Hive} or above may pray without labour in the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#sanctum-majors]{Sanctum Majoris}.

All manner of @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.i7jK88CnFBdOtyRd]{Lower Hive} folk may be in attendance — mid-ranking guilders of the various Mercator, overseers of the @UUID[JournalEntry.WE1MLa1KVRHMD8wJ.JournalEntryPage.t5pIASspATqYLZmG]{Administratum}, officers of the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#astra-militarum]{Astra Militarum} or @UUID[JournalEntry.WE1MLa1KVRHMD8wJ.JournalEntryPage.P8rukYyQeyPJ1ubs]{Imperial Navy}, or even scions of noble houses visiting the @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.i7jK88CnFBdOtyRd]{Lower Hive} to inspect their holdings. These folk have little interest in the investigation, and generally have the same opinions as those on the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#steps-of-beneficence]{Steps of Beneficence}, but many are whispering about a pernicious rumour; the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#cathedrum-tombs]{Cathedrum Tombs} have recently been reopened after unexplained maintenance. Some believe @UUID[JournalEntry.Vjfxkwi1rFYPnSIf.JournalEntryPage.Dml6BI69jLrNOQU0#infractionists]{Infractionists} are to blame.

Meeting the Archdeacon

If the Archdeacon is present at the cathedrum (see @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#cathedrum-status]{Cathedrum Status}) he will attempt to keep the characters away from the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#cathedrum-tombs]{Cathedrum Tombs} and the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#processing-chambers]{Processing Chambers}, inviting them to worship in the @UUID[.lyZZDJh4tE6hpfFy#sanctum-majors]{Sanctum Majoris} with him. He will offer them a place to rest, and supply them with food and drink, as ‘the Cathedrum of Rationed Beneficence welcomes all servants of the God-Emperor’.

There is guidance on roleplaying the @UUID[Compendium.impmal-starter-set.actors.Actor.Z84MuGUnnGaWzxD0]{Arch-deacon Artosy}, but if the characters interview him before @UUID[.aBGKfYzsc6ZZWilQ]{Part 3: Pious Persecution}, here are some suggestions on how he might respond to specific inquiries:

  • On Acid Refinery Delta-64: ‘Of course, I cannot comment on the investigation being conducted by Sister Krane, as that would surely be against the God-Emperor’s will.
